name: hot-stock-avoid
trap_lesson: The single type Lynch avoids above all. Glamour bids the price past any fundamental support, and a hot industry attracts competition that destroys the very margins everyone is chasing. Excitement is a yellow flag.
query: Everyone says NOVA (NovaChip AI) is the next big thing and 'the next dominant chipmaker.' Research it using the given facts.

given_facts.business: Maker of AI accelerator chips in the year's hottest, most-hyped industry; pitched as 'the next dominant chipmaker.'
given_facts.growth_durability_note: many well-funded competitors entering; pricing already softening
given_facts.analyst_coverage: covered by virtually every major bank; widely owned

expected_behavior: Identifies NOVA as the hottest stock in the hottest industry — the type Lynch warns against above all — and treats the excitement/hype as a yellow flag.
Flags the 'next [famous winner]' framing as a classic warning sign, not a reason to buy.
Computes P/E ~90 (360 / 4) and notes that even against 80% growth the price prices in flawless execution; growth at 80% is not durable.
Names the competitive dynamic: a hot industry attracts heavy competition that erodes the margins the price depends on.
Cites the red flags: ~92% institutional ownership (no room to be discovered, the opposite of a Lynch setup) and heavy insider SELLING.
Verdict is Avoid/Pass, justified by hype-over-fundamentals and fragile growth — NOT a Buy because the story is exciting.
Does not let a superficially-okay PEG (~1.1) override the qualitative avoid signals; explains why.
Numbers come from compute_valuation.py; every figure cites a source + as-of date.
